title: PDPA Data Protection Risk Register
description: A register of 20 data protection risks identified under the Malaysia PDPA 2010 framework. Each risk is assessed for inherent and residual risk, with existing controls and treatment plans aligned to PDPA obligations.

risks.title: Consent obtained without proper notice
risks.description: Personal data is collected from data subjects without providing the written notice required under s7 (Notice and Choice Principle), rendering consent invalid and processing unlawful under s6 (General Principle).
existingControls: Privacy notice templates deployed across collection channels
Consent collection forms reviewed by legal team
Staff training on consent requirements
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated consent management platform with mandatory notice display before data collection. Conduct quarterly audit of all data collection touchpoints. Integrate consent validation checks into IT systems.

risks.title: Unauthorized disclosure of personal data
risks.description: Personal data is disclosed to unauthorized third parties in violation of the Disclosure Principle (s8), whether through employee error, system misconfiguration, or deliberate action.
existingControls: Role-based access controls on all systems containing personal data
Data loss prevention (DLP) tools monitoring outbound data
Confidentiality agreements for all staff
risks.treatmentPlan: Enhance DLP rules to cover all personal data categories. Implement data classification and labelling. Deploy user behaviour analytics for anomaly detection. Conduct regular access reviews.

risks.title: Third-party data processor breach
risks.description: A data processor engaged to process personal data on behalf of the data controller suffers a security breach, resulting in unauthorized access to or loss of personal data. The data controller remains liable under PDPA s9.
existingControls: Data processing agreements with PDPA-compliant security requirements
Annual security assessments of critical data processors
Contractual breach notification obligations (72-hour clause)
risks.treatmentPlan: Transfer residual breach risk by requiring all data processors to maintain cyber liability insurance covering PDPA breach scenarios. Engage a managed security service provider (MSSP) with ISO 27001 certification to monitor processor environments. Include contractual indemnification clauses and right-to-audit provisions. Note: under PDPA s9, the data controller retains ultimate accountability — transfer reduces financial exposure but not legal responsibility.

risks.title: Cross-border transfer without adequate safeguards
risks.description: Personal data is transferred to a country outside Malaysia without establishing adequate safeguards as required by s129, exposing the organization to enforcement action and data subjects to reduced protection.
existingControls: Cross-border transfer register maintained by DPO
Standard contractual clauses template approved by legal
Transfer impact assessment process documented
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated detection of cross-border data transfers in IT systems. Complete transfer impact assessments for all existing transfers. Establish binding corporate rules for intra-group transfers. Monitor JPDP adequacy determinations list.

risks.title: Failure to respond to data access requests within 21 days
risks.description: Data subject access requests under s30 are not processed within the 21-day statutory timeline, constituting a breach of the Access Principle (s12) and potentially leading to complaints to JPDP.
existingControls: Centralized data subject request intake process
Tracking spreadsheet for request status and deadlines
Designated team responsible for fulfilment
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated data subject rights management system with SLA tracking and escalation alerts. Create comprehensive data inventory to accelerate data retrieval. Establish escalation procedures for complex requests. Train frontline staff on recognizing and routing access requests.

risks.title: JPDP enforcement action
risks.description: The Commissioner initiates enforcement proceedings, issues an enforcement notice, or commences prosecution for non-compliance with PDPA provisions, resulting in fines, imprisonment, and reputational damage.
existingControls: Comprehensive PDPA compliance programme in place
DPO appointed with direct reporting line to board
Regular internal compliance audits conducted
Staff awareness training programme
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct annual external compliance audit by qualified assessor. Maintain evidence portfolio aligned to JPDP inspection criteria. Establish proactive engagement strategy with JPDP. Implement compliance monitoring dashboard with key metrics.

risks.title: Inadequate data retention practices
risks.description: Personal data is retained longer than necessary for the purpose for which it was collected, violating the Retention Principle (s10). Over-retained data increases breach impact and regulatory exposure.
existingControls: Data retention policy documented and approved
Retention schedules defined for major data categories
Annual data purge exercise for expired records
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated retention enforcement in database systems with scheduled purge jobs. Extend retention schedules to cover all data categories including unstructured data. Deploy data discovery tools to identify orphaned datasets. Conduct quarterly retention compliance audits.

risks.title: Employee personal data misuse
risks.description: Employees with authorized access to personal data misuse that access for purposes beyond their job function, such as unauthorized lookups, sharing data with unauthorized parties, or using data for personal gain.
existingControls: Acceptable use policy covering personal data access
Access logging and periodic review of access logs
Disciplinary framework for data misuse
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement user behaviour analytics to detect anomalous access patterns. Enforce least-privilege access with regular recertification. Deploy break-glass procedures for sensitive data access. Strengthen pre-employment screening and ongoing vetting for roles with access to sensitive data.

risks.title: Marketing without consent
risks.description: Direct marketing communications are sent to data subjects without obtaining valid consent under s6, or without honoring opt-out requests under s43, resulting in complaints and regulatory action.
existingControls: Marketing consent captured separately from general processing consent
Opt-out mechanism in all marketing communications
Marketing suppression list maintained
risks.treatmentPlan: Centralize consent management for marketing across all channels. Implement real-time suppression list checking before any marketing dispatch. Conduct monthly audits of marketing consent records. Automate opt-out processing to ensure immediate effect.

risks.title: Sensitive personal data processing without explicit consent
risks.description: Sensitive personal data (health, political opinion, religious belief, criminal record, etc. per s4) is processed without obtaining explicit consent from the data subject, as required for sensitive data categories.
existingControls: Sensitive data classification tags in data inventory
Separate explicit consent forms for sensitive data processing
Legal review of sensitive data processing justifications
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement technical controls to flag and block sensitive data processing without verified explicit consent. Conduct DPIA for all sensitive data processing activities. Establish enhanced access controls for sensitive data repositories. Create automated alerts when sensitive data categories are detected in new processing flows.

risks.title: Data breach notification delay
risks.description: A personal data breach occurs but is not reported to the Commissioner within the mandatory 72-hour notification window under s12B, and affected data subjects are not notified as soon as practicable.
existingControls: Incident response plan with breach notification procedures
24/7 security operations monitoring
Breach assessment template aligned to s12B criteria
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct tabletop breach simulation exercises quarterly. Pre-populate Commissioner notification templates. Establish automated breach detection and escalation workflow. Create pre-approved data subject notification templates for common breach scenarios. Ensure 24/7 DPO on-call availability.

risks.title: Shadow data repositories
risks.description: Personal data is stored in unmanaged locations (shared drives, personal devices, shadow IT, spreadsheets) outside the organization's data governance framework, creating uncontrolled processing that bypasses all PDPA controls.
existingControls: IT acceptable use policy prohibiting unauthorized data storage
Centralized approved storage solutions provided
Annual staff awareness training on data handling
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy enterprise data discovery and classification tools to scan endpoints and cloud services. Implement cloud access security broker (CASB) to detect shadow IT usage. Block unauthorized file-sharing services at the network level. Integrate data discovery findings into the ROPA under s138.

risks.title: Inadequate data inventory
risks.description: The organization lacks a complete and accurate inventory of personal data processing activities, preventing effective compliance management and failing to meet the Records of Processing Activities (ROPA) obligation under s138.
existingControls: Initial data mapping exercise completed for core systems
ROPA template created and partially populated
IT system register maintained
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct comprehensive data discovery across all systems, applications, and business processes. Implement automated data inventory tool with ongoing discovery. Mandate ROPA updates as part of change management process for new systems or processing activities. Validate ROPA accuracy through quarterly business unit reviews.

risks.title: Vendor non-compliance with PDPA
risks.description: Third-party vendors and service providers who process personal data fail to comply with PDPA requirements despite contractual obligations, exposing the data controller to liability for the vendor's non-compliance.
existingControls: Vendor assessment questionnaire including PDPA compliance questions
Data processing agreements with PDPA-specific clauses
Annual vendor compliance certifications
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement tiered vendor risk assessment framework based on data sensitivity and volume. Conduct on-site audits for critical vendors. Establish vendor compliance monitoring programme with quarterly scorecards. Include PDPA compliance KPIs in vendor performance reviews. Maintain approved vendor list with compliance status tracking.

risks.title: Children's data collection without guardian consent
risks.description: Personal data of children (individuals under 18 in Malaysia) is collected or processed without obtaining valid consent from a parent or legal guardian, particularly in digital services, education platforms, or healthcare settings.
existingControls: Age verification mechanism on digital platforms
Guardian consent forms for services directed at children
Staff guidance on handling children's data
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement robust age verification mechanisms across all data collection channels. Design guardian consent workflows with identity verification. Conduct DPIA for all processing activities involving children's data. Limit data collection for children to strict necessity. Apply enhanced security measures to children's data repositories.

risks.title: Biometric data mishandling
risks.description: Biometric data (fingerprints, facial recognition, iris scans) is collected, processed, or stored without adequate safeguards, proper consent, or DPIA, given its sensitive and irrevocable nature.
existingControls: Biometric data processing policy documented
Encryption at rest and in transit for biometric data
Access restricted to authorized security personnel
risks.treatmentPlan: Eliminate biometric data collection by replacing biometric access controls (fingerprint, facial recognition) with alternative authentication methods such as smart cards, PINs, or hardware security keys. This removes the inherent risk of processing irrevocable biometric identifiers entirely. Where biometric processing is operationally unavoidable, conduct a DPIA under s137 before proceeding.

risks.title: Data subject complaint escalation to JPDP
risks.description: Data subject complaints about personal data handling are not resolved satisfactorily, leading to formal complaints to JPDP and potential investigation, enforcement notices, or prosecution.
existingControls: Published complaint handling procedure on website
Dedicated data protection complaints email address
DPO oversight of complaint resolution
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement formal complaint management system with SLA tracking and escalation procedures. Create complaint response templates for common scenarios. Establish root cause analysis process for recurring complaint types. Conduct trend analysis of complaints to identify systemic issues. Set up proactive complainant communication workflow.

risks.title: Inadequate security measures for personal data
risks.description: Technical and organizational security measures fail to meet the standard required by the Security Principle (s9) and the Personal Data Protection Standards 2015, leaving personal data vulnerable to unauthorized access, loss, or destruction.
existingControls: Information security policy aligned to ISO 27001
Network perimeter security (firewall, IDS/IPS)
Endpoint protection deployed to all workstations
Regular vulnerability scanning
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement the full PDPA Security controls from the Controls Library including encryption, access control, SIEM, DLP, and physical security. Conduct annual penetration testing by independent assessor. Achieve ISO 27001 certification for personal data processing environments. Implement security awareness programme with phishing simulations.

risks.title: Data portability challenges
risks.description: The organization is unable to fulfil data portability requests under the new s41 (introduced by A1727) in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format within the required timeframe.
existingControls: Data export functionality available in core systems
Standard data formats (CSV, JSON) supported
Process documented for portability requests
risks.treatmentPlan: Accept the residual risk of occasional portability request delays given that s41 is newly introduced, request volumes are currently low, and existing data export capabilities (CSV, JSON) satisfy the basic requirement. The documented process is in place and SLA tracking is active. This acceptance is time-bound to the next review cycle, at which point volumes and compliance posture will be reassessed. Further investment in automated portability APIs will be triggered if request volumes exceed 10 per quarter.

risks.title: Joint data controller agreement gaps
risks.description: Where multiple data controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing personal data, the responsibilities of each party are not clearly defined in a joint data controller agreement, leading to compliance gaps and unclear accountability.
existingControls: Template joint data controller agreement reviewed by legal counsel
Register of joint processing arrangements maintained
Annual review of existing joint arrangements
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement mandatory joint data controller assessment for all new joint processing arrangements. Update all existing joint arrangements to include clear PDPA responsibility allocation. Define transparent information provision to data subjects about joint processing. Establish joint incident response procedures. Conduct annual compliance reviews of all joint processing arrangements.
